step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("what does EDTA do in dental procedures")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First state if you agree/disagree with the initial conclusion, then give one sentence justification based on the evidence. Finally present your selected letter choice in boxed notation.
I agree with the initial conclusion that EDTA does not cause pulp fixation. The retrieved literature confirms EDTA functions primarily as a chelating agent that binds calcium ions and facilitates removal of the smear layer during dental procedures, yet there is no mention of any mechanism by which EDTA stabilizes or fixes pulp tissue. Thus, the claim that EDTA causes pulp fixation lacks scientific foundation and contradicts established dental pharmacology.
